content:
{
  "title": "Adversarial Contextual Priming: Prompt Preambles for LLM Robustness",
  "problem": "Large language models are vulnerable to adversarial prompt injection and jailbreak attacks that bypass alignment safeguards. Existing prompting methods often fail to maintain robustness under such attacks.",
  "motivation": "Ensuring LLMs remain robust against adversarial manipulation is critical as these models see increased real-world deployment. Traditional prompt engineering and fine-tuning struggle with the breadth and novelty of adversarial attacks. Prompt-time defenses could provide a practical and deployable first line of defense.",
  "method": "We propose Adversarial Contextual Priming, a prompting method in which a short, non-intrusive preamble is prepended to each user prompt. This preamble contains explicit meta-instructions to recognize and resist adversarial features, plus high-entropy, task-irrelevant distractors to reduce susceptibility to prompt injection. The preamble is dynamically varied per session to prevent overfitting of attack techniques. The method is lightweight and model-agnostic, requiring no model retraining.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on jailbreak/adversarial prompt datasets such as AdvBench and JailbreakBench, measuring attack success rates with and without the method. Baselines include vanilla prompting and static instruction preambles. Metrics: attack success rate reduction, impact on helpfulness/harmlessness, and latency."
}
